ISO 42001 / NIST AI RMF alignment: ISO 42001 AI management system and continual improvement; NIST Govern
Implementation notes: Use managed settings for non-negotiable controls such as allowManagedHooksOnly and managed permission rules.

Primary control: Deterministic deny rules for .env, secrets/, SSH, cloud creds, system credential paths
Main risk addressed: Sensitive information disclosure; prompt injection leading to secret exposure
ISO 42001 / NIST AI RMF alignment: NIST Map data access and Manage risk treatment
Implementation notes: Prefer explicit deny rules over advisory ignore patterns. Use deny, ask, allow with source visibility in /permissions.

Primary control: cc-redact or equivalent managed redaction hook
Implementation notes: Allow structural visibility where needed, but never expose live values. Keep redaction as a usability layer, not the only security control.

Primary control: Deny rules for curl, wget, nc, sudo, su, rm -rf, git push --force
ISO 42001 / NIST AI RMF alignment: NIST Manage with least privilege and constrained actions
Implementation notes: Block known-bad commands without prompting. Reserve prompts for bounded medium-risk commands only.

Primary control: Managed PreToolUse Bash hook inspecting full command strings
ISO 42001 / NIST AI RMF alignment: ISO 42001 control effectiveness; NIST Measure and Manage
Implementation notes: Use hooks to inspect full Bash payloads for chaining, decode-and-execute, and mixed read-plus-egress patterns.

Primary control: Sandbox domain allowlist; deny generic download/exfiltration tools
Main risk addressed: Secret exfiltration; supply chain and prompt-driven misuse
ISO 42001 / NIST AI RMF alignment: NIST Map external dependencies and Manage exposure
Implementation notes: Use sandbox network controls and approved domains such as GitHub and package registries instead of broad shell-based network access.

Primary control: Sandboxed allow-read / allow-write plus deny-read / deny-write paths
Main risk addressed: Access outside intended workspace; local secret discovery
ISO 42001 / NIST AI RMF alignment: NIST Map system context and Manage boundaries
Implementation notes: Sandboxing provides OS-layer filesystem isolation and reduces prompt frequency while preserving explicit deny behavior.

Primary control: Deny writes to .git/ and .husky/; deny force push; ask for higher-impact GitHub actions
ISO 42001 / NIST AI RMF alignment: ISO 42001 accountability and change control; NIST Govern
Implementation notes: Allow low-risk read operations, prompt for consequential actions, and block irreversible or control-bypassing actions.

Primary control: Use allowlists for safe repetitive actions plus sandbox auto-allow within boundaries
Main risk addressed: Approval fatigue and unsafe click-through behavior
ISO 42001 / NIST AI RMF alignment: ISO 42001 continual improvement; NIST Measure and Manage
Implementation notes: Tune ask-to-allow over time using telemetry while keeping hard denies stable.

ISO 42001 / NIST AI RMF alignment: ISO 42001 governance and awareness; NIST Govern
Implementation notes: CLAUDE.md gives behavioral guidance; hooks and settings provide deterministic enforcement.

Primary control: /permissions, hook logs, approval/denial trends, exception review
Main risk addressed: Control drift; excessive prompts; weak exceptions
Implementation notes: Review recurring prompts, denied commands, and exception requests to refine controls without weakening the hard boundary.
